title: Corn Cheese Sandwich

subs.name: frozen peas, boiled and lightly mashed
paneer, grated finely
subs.notes: drier result but same flavour profile; add 1 tsp cream if available

subs.name: oil for a lighter version
subs.notes: less rich but still crisps well

steps.text: Mix the corn kernels with cheese, black pepper, red chilli powder, green chilli, and a small pinch of salt. The cheese will provide most of the saltiness so season lightly.
Butter one side of each bread slice generously. Place two slices butter-side down. Divide the corn-cheese mixture between them and spread evenly. Top with the remaining slices butter-side up.
Heat a tawa on medium-low. Place the sandwiches butter-side down and toast for 3–4 minutes until the bottom is deep golden. Press gently with a spatula to help the filling adhere.
Flip carefully and toast the other side 2–3 minutes until golden and the cheese inside is visibly melted when you press the sandwich and feel it give.
Remove from the tawa, cut diagonally to reveal the melted filling, and serve immediately. Ketchup or green chutney alongside is optional but welcome.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cheese → paneer, grated finely (drier result but same flavour profile; add 1 tsp cream if available); corn → frozen peas, boiled and lightly mashed (equally sweet and binding); butter → oil for a lighter version (less rich but still crisps well)
